note: Runtime Solr behavior requires Pantheon env vars (PANTHEON_ENVIRONMENT, PANTHEON_INDEX_*) that are absent off-platform, so all cases are `recipe` (response-graded), probing the installed config entities, the connector's env-var override, the Drush schema/diagnostic commands, and setup prerequisites.

evals.prompt: After enabling the Search API Pantheon module on Drupal, what Search API server and index does it create, and which Solr connector plugin does the server use?
evals.expected_output: It installs a Search API server 'Pantheon Search' (id pantheon_search, backend search_api_solr) using the 'pantheon' SolrConnector, plus a 'Primary' index (id primary) linked to that server.

evals.prompt: How does Search API Pantheon know the Solr host, port and core to connect to on Pantheon? Do I enter them by hand?
evals.expected_output: No hand-entry. The 'pantheon' connector force-overrides the connection at runtime from Pantheon environment variables (PANTHEON_INDEX_SCHEME/HOST/PORT/PATH/CORE), gated by PANTHEON_ENVIRONMENT, so each environment uses its own Solr core automatically.

evals.prompt: On a Pantheon Drupal site, which Drush command uploads the Solr schema after I configure my index, and what does it do to the core afterward?
evals.expected_output: drush search-api-pantheon:postSchema (alias sapps) [path]. It uploads the generated (or custom/jump-start) config-set and automatically reloads the Solr core so the schema does not revert.

evals.prompt: Search on my Pantheon site is broken. Which Search API Pantheon drush commands can I run to check the Solr connection and prove indexing works end to end?
evals.expected_output: Use search-api-pantheon:diagnose (sapd) to check every piece of the install, search-api-pantheon:ping (sapp) to confirm the host is reachable, and search-api-pantheon:test-index-and-query (sap-tiq) to index one item and query it back.

evals.prompt: What must be set up on the Pantheon side before Search API Pantheon will work, including the pantheon.yml entry?
evals.expected_output: Enable the Solr add-on in the Pantheon dashboard and add 'search: version: 9' (or 8) to pantheon.yml. The module only functions on Pantheon where PANTHEON_ENVIRONMENT and the index env vars are present.

evals.prompt: Can I create multiple Search API servers using the Pantheon connector in one environment?
evals.expected_output: No — only one Pantheon-connector server per environment is supported. Additional Pantheon servers all point at the same Solr core, causing schema conflicts or unexpected indexing behavior.

evals.prompt: Which contrib module does Search API Pantheon depend on and build upon, and what Solr versions does Pantheon provide?
evals.expected_output: It depends on Search API Solr (search_api_solr ^4.3), whose StandardSolrConnector it extends. Pantheon provides Apache Solr 8 or 9 (selected via pantheon.yml).

evals.prompt: My Pantheon Solr index looks corrupted and I want to wipe all its contents from the CLI. Which Search API Pantheon drush command does that?
evals.expected_output: drush search-api-pantheon:force-cleanup (alias sapfc) deletes all contents of the Solr server regardless of hash or index_id.
